channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Iran hits second Kuwait power plant, Bahrain repels aerial attacks
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Iran strikes another Kuwaiti power plant, causing outages and fires. Bahrain also repels aerial attacks amidst escalating regional tensions.
Read More: https://punchng.com/iran-hits-second-kuwait-power-plant-bahrain-repels-aerial-attacks/

item.title: Jigawa digitises informal revenue collection to curb leakages
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Jigawa State adopts a digital platform to automate informal revenue collection, enhancing transparency and curbing leakages. Learn more about this initiati
Read More: https://punchng.com/jigawa-digitises-informal-revenue-collection-to-curb-leakages/

item.title: Viral photograph of female kidnappers in Rivers is AI-generated – Police
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Rivers State Police dismiss viral photo of female kidnappers as AI-generated, urging public to disregard the false information and avoid panic.
Read More: https://punchng.com/viral-photograph-of-female-kidnappers-in-rivers-is-ai-generated-police/

item.title: Police arrest three Bayelsa workers for stealing N500m govt equipment
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa State Police have arrested three ministry staff for allegedly stealing N500m worth of government equipment, including a generator and forklift. Rea
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-three-bayelsa-workers-for-stealing-n500m-govt-equipment/

item.title: NPFL: Niger Tornadoes line up two friendlies in Bida
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Niger Tornadoes will intensify preparations for new season with two warm-up games in Bida, DAILY POST reports. The Ikon Allah Boys will do battle with Bida Selected and Bida Lions on Saturday and Sunday respectively. The matches will be played at the Aliyu Ndayeko Memorial Stadium. The friendly matc

item.title: NPFL: Doma United, Wikki Tourists clash in pre-season friendly
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Doma United will lock horns with Nigeria National League, NNL, side Wikki Tourists in a warm-up game ahead of the 2026-27 season. The encounter is billed for the Abubakar Tafawa Balewa stadium on Saturday. The game is expected to be used to assess players readiness and finetune strategies for the ne

item.title: White House backs Argentina players over Falklands banner in World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: White House Fifa taskforce chief defends Argentina footballers, saying US believes in free speechThe White House has backed Argentina’s footballers who displayed a banner supporting their country’s claim to the Falklands Islands after their World Cup semi-final victory against England.In a fractious

item.title: Why is Latin America shifting to the right?
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description:
🇨🇴 🇵🇪 #Colombia and #Peru recently elected right-wing, #Trump-aligned leaders in close contests, which analysts link to weakening centrist parties and growing concern over crime rather than a clear ideological shift.
🇧🇷 Trump has amplified the trend by hosting regional allies in Florida, though hi

item.title: Student Assault: Bauchi Police Urge Vigilance After Three Suspects Arrested
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has called on parents, guardians and school authorities to strengthen vigilance around schools following the arrest of three suspects linked to the machete attack on a secondary school student. The Command said the 18-year-old student of Government Day Secondary Schoo

item.title: ‘Bringing Buhari back to power worst thing done to Nigeria’ – Sam Amadi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Political analyst and Director of the Abuja School of Social and Political Thought, Dr. Sam Amadi, has described late former President Muhammadu Buhari’s return to office in 2015 as “the worst thing” that happened to Nigeria. Amadi made the remark in a post on his X handle on Satur

item.title: Official: Super Eagles star completes move to Russia Premier League club Rostov
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles forward Olakunle Olusegun has completed a permanent move to Russian Premier League club FC Rostov, ending his five-year association with FC Krasnodar, Soccernet.ng reports. The transfer was officially confirmed on Friday, July 17, with Rostov signing the 24-year-old winger on a three-ye

item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Full List: US places 23 countries on highest ‘Do Not Travel’ alert
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The United States Department of State has updated its global travel guidance, placing 23 countries under its highest travel warning, Level 4, advising American citizens not to visit the listed destinations under any circumstances. The post Full List: US places 23 countries on highest ‘Do Not Travel’

item.title: There may be terrorists’ agents in Nigerian Army, Police – Presidency
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu’s Special Adviser on Policy Communication, Daniel Bwala, has hinted that there may be agents of terrorists in the Nigerian Army and Nigeria Police Force, NPF. Bwala made the remark on The Link Up Podcast, a panel discussion hosted by EchoRoom and published on Friday, whil

item.title: Ekiti monthly IGR rises to N2.7bn without new taxes – Official
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The official attributed the achievement to improved voluntary tax compliance, digital tax administration, public enlightenment, tax reforms, an empowered workforce, as well as the enabling environment created by the administration of Governor Biodun Oyebanji. The post Ekiti monthly IGR rises to N2.7

item.title: Trump Questions England’s World Cup Tactics
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The US president on Friday took a gentle swipe at coach Thomas Tuchel's decision to adopt defensive tactics after England took the lead against Argentina on Wednesday. The post Trump Questions England’s World Cup Tactics appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Kidnapped Nasarawa varsity professor rescued, suspects arrested
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Operatives of the Department of State Services, DSS, have rescued a lecturer at the Nasarawa State University, Keffi, NSUK, Professor Samuel Okunsebor, who was abducted by gunmen. Okunsebor, the Dean, Faculty of Agriculture at the university, was said to have been abducted on Wednesday at Mile Uku,

item.title: World Cup winner Capdevila appeals to Trump after visa denial
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Former Spain international Joan Capdevila asks US President Donald Trump for help after he was denied a visa to enter the United States before Sunday's World Cup final.

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Are England facing the prospect of using a false nine up front once record scorer Harry Kane calls time on his international career?

item.title: Troops rescue kidnap victims in Abia, recover hijacked truck
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the 14 Brigade, Nigerian Army, operating under Operation Eastern Sanity II, have rescued kidnapped victims, recovered a hijacked truck and arrested suspected IPOB/ESN gunmen. This was disclosed in a statement by the Assistant Director Army Public Relations,14 Brigade Nigerian Army Ohafia,

item.title: 2027: Obi Clean Record Can Not Be Matched By Other Presidential Candidates – Odoh

item.title: Truecaller Q2 Revenue Falls 21% as Advertising Weakness Persists
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Caller identification platform Truecaller reported a 21 per cent year-on-year decline in second-quarter revenue as continued weakness in its advertising business weighed on earnings, despite strong growth in subscriptions, enterprise services, and its global user base, which signalled improving mome

item.title: Cross River: Akparabong Women Protest Drug Peddling, Demand NDLEA Action
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A major drug crisis has hit Akparabong community in Ikom local government area of Cross River State, forcing hundreds of women basically mothers to take to the streets to demand urgent intervention from the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency, NDLEA. The women, who said they could no longer keep mu

item.title: Aston Villa Sign Swiss World Cup Star Manzambi
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Midfielder Manzambi arrives after an impressive World Cup campaign in which he scored three times and provided two assists in four games before suffering an injury. The post Aston Villa Sign Swiss World Cup Star Manzambi appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Nigeria’s fuel puzzle deepens as imports surge, refinery output falls
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigeria’s importation of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS), popularly known as petrol, increased by 207 percent in June 2026, according to new data released by the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority (NMDPRA).

item.title: Mary Habila: Police spokesperson warns against allegations, urges restraints
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Uchenna Inya, Abakaliki The spokesperson of the Nigeria Police Force Headquarters Annex, Lagos, CSP Nkeiruka Nwode, has appealed to Nigerians to exercise restraint and refrain from spreading unverified allegations over the death of Miss Mary Habila. Habila died at the residence of the Minister

item.title: I will reverse naming of public assets after Tinubu if elected President – Sowore vows
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: African Action Congress, AAC, presidential candidate, Omoyele Sowore, has declared that he would reverse all public facilities named after President Bola Tinubu if he’s elected Nigeria’s President in 2027. Sowore condemned the naming of highways, public institutions and other national as

item.title: Panic As Residents Discover Lifeless Body In Calabar Water Channel
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Panic has spread through Elijah Henshaw Street off Palm Street in Calabar South local government area of Cross River State, after residents discovered a lifeless body inside a water channel. The body was discovered on Friday, July 17, 2026, amid heavy rainfall that has continued to flood parts of Ca

item.title: Cooking gas imports jump by 1400% as prices reduce in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Liquefied Petroleum Gas, LPG, otherwise known as cooking gas, imported into Nigeria jumped sharply by 1,400 percent to 1.5 kilotonnes per day in June 2026. This is according to the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority, NMDPRA, Fact Sheet for June released on Friday. The r

It was gathered that five officers of the Service, including a Deputy Comptroller, have been quizzed as part of the ongoin

item.title: Fuel import rises 207% as Nigeria’s daily consumption hits 50.6 ml/d
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigeria’s Premium Motor Spirit, PMS, import jumped by 207 per cent to 18.1 million litres per day in June 2026 from 5.6 million litres per day in May as daily petrol consumption surged by 7 per cent to 50.6 ml/d. This is according to the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory A

item.title: Nigeria taps banks, local capital to close $171bn climate finance gap

item.description: Nigeria is turning to domestic financial institutions and private capital to bridge a staggering $171 billion climate financing gap, as read more Nigeria taps banks, local capital to close $171bn climate finance gap

item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Opta’s supercomputer has predicted Spain to defeat defending champions Argentina in Sunday’s 2026 FIFA World Cup final, giving La Roja a 59 per cent chance of lifting the trophy. According to TNT Sports, the data model rates Spain as favourites to win the title in New Jersey, while Argen

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Federal Government has denied reports that it plans to ban the export of raw cocoa beans, stressing that its priority is to promote value addition across Nigeria's cocoa industry rather than restrict exports. The post FG denies plan to ban raw cocoa exports, pushes value addition appeared first

item.title: Fresh US-Canada showdown looms as Trump makes new threat
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description:
US President Donald Trump has threatened to increase tariffs on Canadian goods, saying the extra charges could help cover the costs caused by wildfire smoke drifting into the United States.
Smoke from hundreds of wildfires burning across Canada and parts of northern Minnesota has spread into severa

The foundation disclosed that the third edition of the

item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Argentina captain Lionel Messi says it is “crazy” to be facing Spain’s Lamine Yamal in Sunday’s World Cup final after being photographed with him as a baby. In 2007 Lamine Yamal’s parents won a charity photoshoot at Barcelona in which 20-year-old Messi held their five-m

item.title: Why Nigerian Money Market Funds are your best emergency fund upgrade
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian Money Market Fund (MMF) market is one to watch if you’re seeking competitive returns while keeping your capital safe and highly liquid. The post Why Nigerian Money Market Funds are your best emergency fund upgrade appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Police Warn Against Indiscriminate Gunfire By Armed Security Personnel
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The Commissioner of Police, Borno State Command, CP Naziru Abdulmajid described the practice as reckless, unprofessional, and dangerous. The post Police Warn Against Indiscriminate Gunfire By Armed Security Personnel appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: More games and more controversy - has World Cup been a success?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: From high ticket prices to hydration breaks, new laws and political interventions, there has rarely been a dull moment at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Argentinian Fans Perform Goodluck Rituals Ahead Of Worldcup Finals
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: The 2026 World Cup final is approaching, and Argentinian fans have turned to spiritual rituals with chants, incense, symbolic offerings, all aimed at boosting their team’s chances. The world cup finals will hold on Sunday July 19, 2026 and Argentina will be playing with Spain.

item.title: Super Eagles Defender Nears Fulham Move
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Trabzonspor defender Chibuike Nwaiwu is closing in on a permanent move to Premier League side Fulham, Completesports.com reports. According to Sky Sport, Fulham have tabled a €27 million bid for the Nigerian centre-back, while Trabzonspor are holding out for €30 million. The two clubs are believed t

item.title: England carry ‘scars’ of World Cup exit, says Tuchel
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "We didn't get the result, so I take, of course, the responsibility for these decisions. But the decisions are made under stress, the decisions are made in-game." The post England carry ‘scars’ of World Cup exit, says Tuchel appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup: Everything you need to know about England vs France match
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup third-place playoff will see England take on France in a battle for the bronze medal after both teams suffered semifinal defeats. The post World Cup: Everything you need to know about England vs France match appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Activist calls for probe of Umahi over Mary Habila’s death
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Kogi-born political activist and public commentator, Comrade Usman Okai Austin, has called on the Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Olatunji Rilwan Disu, to ensure a thorough and transparent investigation into the death of 26-year-old physiotherapist, Mary Habila.

item.title: 2027: Running-mate battle heats up as INEC opens gov’ship candidates’ portal
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The battle for deputy governorship slots has intensified across the states as political parties move to upload the names and read more 2027: Running-mate battle heats up as INEC opens gov’ship candidates’ portal

item.title: How Shakira and Burna Boy Are Preparing for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Halftime Show With The Ghetto Kids
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Since its establishment in 1930, no FIFA World Cup game or final has ever had a halftime show. Under the Laws of the Game set by the International Football Association Board (IFAB), which FIFA follows, players are entitled to a half-time interval that must not exceed 15 minutes. The rule emphasises

item.title: The strategic impact of leadership in dialing up business performance
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Every organisation encounters challenges. Sometimes the challenges are so fundamental that they prompt difficult questions: How did we get here? What went wrong? Who did what, when, why and how? What happens next? In the midst of these questions, leadership becomes the defining factor between declin

item.title: Ruthless All Blacks prove too strong for Ireland at Eden Park
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: A ruthless All Blacks display ensured they will end their home block of rugby's inaugural Nations Championship with three wins from three, after emphatically beating Ireland 40-21 at Eden Park.

item.title: FCT Getting Better Under Wike 50 Years After Creation —Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has praised the performance of the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, saying Abuja is witnessing significant transformation under his leadership, 50 years after the creation of the nation’s capital. Tinubu made the remarks at a special thanksgiving di

item.title: Imo unapologetically a one party State — Gov Uzodimma
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: …Insists Tinubu stands on solid ground of performance for re-election in 2027 By Romanus Ugwu The governor of Imo State, Hope Uzodimma, has boasted that the state is unrepentantly a one party state to massive vote President Bola Tinubu in the next year’s presidential election. He spoke w

item.title: Coronation Merchant Bank names Obeahon Ohiwerei as CEO
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Coronation Merchant Bank has appointed Obeahon Ohiwerei as its new Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer, marking a leadership change read more Coronation Merchant Bank names Obeahon Ohiwerei as CEO

item.title: US Democrats divided over support for Israel ahead of midterm elections
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US House Democrats were divided into a near-even split by a vote this week over whether to cut military aid to Israel, which was defeated through almost total opposition from the Republican party. Ahead of November's midterm elections, the vote reflects a sharp erosion in support for Israel among le
